name: Malaysia
description: Malaysia is Asia's most accessible study destination, offering quality education at affordable costs. Its multicultural environment and English medium instruction make international students feel at home.

tuitionFees.undergraduate: Flexible study options - Kuching. Melbourne. Or both! Student will pay only 1/3 the tuition fee as a Swinburne Sarawak student
tuitionFees.phd: MYR 10,000–25,000 per year; research scholarships available

requirements: Visa approval letter from Malaysian immigration
Medical examination at a government-registered clinic
Student Pass application through your university

workPermit.postStudy: Graduate Employment Pass available; Malaysia My Second Home (MM2H) for long-term stay

languageRequirements.notes: English medium instruction; IELTS 6.0+ for top universities

faqs.question: Is it easy to get a student visa for Malaysia?
faqs.answer: With proper documentation and financial proof, obtaining a student visa for Malaysia is straightforward. MVR Consultants can guide you through the entire process.

faqs.question: What are the tuition fees in Malaysia?
faqs.answer: Tuition fees vary by university and program. Contact our counselors for program-specific fee structures.

faqs.question: Can I work while studying?
faqs.answer: Most countries allow international students to work part-time during term time. Specific hours and conditions depend on the country's immigration rules.

faqs.question: Are there scholarships available for Indian students?
faqs.answer: Yes, many universities and governments offer merit-based and need-based scholarships for international students. Ask our counselors for scholarship matching.
